title: Structured importer for CIViC/Open Targets (assistive, flag-on-doubt)
context: The structured importer maps approved source records into typed, provenance-bearing assertions; extraction is assistive and never invents. Only open / public-domain / openly-licensed sources (CIViC CC0, Open Targets CC0, Reactome, license-checked PMC open-access subset); COSMIC, OncoKB, DrugBank, any non-open DB and all controlled-access / patient-level data (dbGaP, EGA, biobanks, EHR) are out of scope and refused. No patient data; all therapeutic-target content is research evidence labeled 'not medical advice'. See PLAN.md.
objective: Build a structured importer that maps CIViC / Open Targets records to typed assertions, preserving native evidence level and release version into provenance and flagging ambiguous fields for human review.
acceptanceCriteria: Maps CIViC / Open Targets records to typed assertions, preserving each source's native evidence level and release version into provenance.
Therapeutic-target assertions auto-carry the 'research evidence — not medical advice' label.
Ambiguous fields are flagged for human review, never guessed.
output: Importer code + tests mapping CIViC/Open Targets to typed assertions, committed via PR.
